counter_adjustments.reason: gauntlet_v1 factory prereg (docs/lab_prereg_gauntlet_v1.md section 8): 90 pre-committed grid cells = 90 hypotheses; new_strategy added +1, this adjustment adds the remaining +89. Survivor child slugs will each carry a -1 adjustment at registration so no cell is ever counted twice.

migrated_from.note: house-lab generalization (operator directive): single registry + single multiple-testing counter; pre-migration file frozen as history

bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: 3bps slippage (tighter than gauntlet_v1's LARGE-bucket 5bps, disclosed judgment call for highly liquid ETFs), $25 min ticket, $10k notional. Prereg-mandated 2x-slippage (6bps) sensitivity on the sole stage-1 survivor (sma_200): IS CAGR 1.03% (still beats benchmark's 0.08%), HO CAGR 1.11% (still loses to benchmark's 3.03%) -- verdict unchanged at 2x.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: signal_lag=1 (identical engine feature/discipline as gauntlet_v1): the selection rule sees bars only through the trading day before the execution day; trades fill at the execution day's own close. No fundamentals, no restatement exposure -- signals are pure price (252-day return, 200-day SMA).
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: 2 trials by design, both counted: hypotheses_ever went from 92 to 93 at prereg (new_strategy +1, cell-count adjustment +1 -- one count per grid cell, same convention as gauntlet_v1). DSR benchmarked against n_trials=2 with variance_of_sr=0.01796 measured on the grid's own 2 in-sample Sharpes. Holdout touched exactly once, by the sole stage-1 survivor (sma_200).
bias_checklist.overfitting: Zero free parameters after the prereg commit: the 252-day and 200-day lookback windows are the canonical textbook values from the cited literature (Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen; Delphi's own 200-day-class MA convention), not tuned to this dataset. Portfolio construction (equal weight among currently-long assets, no top-N) has no free knob. abs_mom_252 failed stage 1 outright; sma_200 cleared stage 1 but then failed stage 2 against the SAME frozen benchmark bar -- the holdout did its job.
bias_checklist.regime: In-sample (2008-08..2017-12) spans the GFC recovery and the low-rate QE decade, where the always-invested benchmark barely made money (0.08%/yr CAGR) and a defensive trend filter had room to add value (sma_200 IS CAGR 1.21%). Holdout (2018-01..2026-06) includes the 2018 vol spike, COVID crash, and 2022 bond bear market, but ALSO a strong multi-year recovery that the always-invested benchmark captured in full (HO CAGR 3.03%) while sma_200's periodic cash exposure captured only part of it (HO CAGR 1.28%) -- the same 'a passive benchmark just did better in this decade' pattern that killed gauntlet_v1's low-vol survivors, now observed in a structurally different asset class and signal family.
bias_checklist.selection: Fixed, named 17-ETF universe (one liquid instrument per rates/credit/commodity/currency/country-equity slot) chosen for category coverage and inception history BEFORE any return was examined -- disclosed as a judgment call, not a performance-driven pick. The 2-cell grid (abs_mom_252, sma_200) was the complete pre-committed catalog; nothing added or removed after the prereg.
bias_checklist.small_n: 215 monthly rebalances 2008-08..2026-06 (~112 in-sample, ~102 holdout); ~2,348 IS and ~2,134 HO daily equity-curve observations per cell for Sharpe/DSR/PSR, using each cell's own n_obs, skew and kurtosis. mean_excess (-0.24%/yr) and shrunk mean (-0.02%/yr, n=2) both sit near zero, honestly reflecting one cell failing badly (abs_mom_252) and one cell narrowly failing only at the holdout stage (sma_200) rather than a uniform result.
bias_checklist.survivorship: Fixed, currently-live, liquid 17-ETF universe -- no delistings in this window (disclosed in the prereg as a scope choice). A real infrastructure bug WAS found and fixed before any signal was computed: Robinhood's historicals endpoint pads dates before a symbol's real listing with a flat, constant-OHLC placeholder bar instead of returning not_found (confirmed on SHY: 418 identical 81.05 bars, volume 0, then a real 2.44M-volume jump on its true 2007-08-31 data-start date). Mechanically stripped per-symbol (open==high==low==close run from bar 0) before any lookback signal was computed -- verified against EEM/FXI, which show genuine day-to-day price variation despite occasional volume=0 records, so the strip correctly left their real history untouched. Now a reusable house utility: shared.historicals.strip_preinception_padding.

backtest.notes: 2-cell grid on a fixed 17-ETF macro universe. Stage 1 (in-sample 2008-08..2017-12): abs_mom_252 DSR=0.00 (fails, also loses to benchmark IS CAGR: -1.48% vs 0.08%); sma_200 DSR=1.00 (passes) and beats benchmark IS CAGR (1.21% vs 0.08%) -- advances alone. Stage 2 (holdout 2018-01..2026-06, once): sma_200 PSR=1.00 (genuinely positive risk-adjusted return) but HO CAGR 1.28% does NOT beat the benchmark's 3.03% -- zero stage-2 passers. Unchanged at 2x slippage. Also produced a reusable house infrastructure fix: shared.historicals.strip_preinception_padding, guarding against Robinhood's flat pre-listing placeholder bars.

cross_asset_trend.falsifiable_claim: At least one of 2 pre-committed cells (252-day absolute momentum; 200-day SMA trend filter) clears DSR>=0.95 (n_trials=2) and beats the always-invested EW benchmark in-sample, then clears PSR>=0.95 and beats the benchmark on the untouched 2018-2026 holdout. Zero passers at either stage refutes.
cross_asset_trend.mechanism: Absolute (time-series) trend-following on macro asset-class ETFs (rates, credit, commodities, currencies, country equity indices): persistent one-directional order flow from non-forecasting actors (pension glide-path rebalancers, corporate hedgers, central banks defending currency bands, sovereign commodity allocators) creates autocorrelation a simple trend rule can ride. Structurally distinct from gauntlet_v1 cross-sectional equity momentum (refuted), whose proposed source was single-company analyst underreaction, not asset-class order flow.

prereg.metric: Net-of-cost monthly-rebalanced daily equity curve per cell (signal_lag=1, execution at next month's first trading-day close); annualized Sharpe -> DSR (n_trials=2) in-sample; PSR on holdout; net CAGR vs the equal-weight always-invested 17-ETF benchmark.
prereg.population_definition: Fixed 17-ETF universe (TLT, IEF, SHY, LQD, HYG, GLD, SLV, USO, DBA, DBB, UUP, FXE, FXY, EEM, EFA, FXI, EWZ) chosen for asset-class category coverage and long inception history, not performance -- one per rates/credit/commodity/currency/country-equity slot. Vol-linked ETPs excluded by design (structural roll-decay confound). No PIT ranking; fixed named list, disclosed judgment call.
prereg.success_criteria: Stage 1 (IS): DSR>=0.95 @ n_trials=2 AND beat EW benchmark IS CAGR -- advance. Stage 2 (holdout, once): PSR>=0.95 AND beat benchmark holdout CAGR -- supported. Refuted iff zero passers at either stage. Inconclusive only for >20% rebalance coverage failure.

cross_asset_trend.underutilized_because: Retail/self-directed capital concentrates almost entirely on single-stock picking (as every prior house study -- Oracle, Midas, Gauntlet -- also did); a 17-ETF absolute-trend overlay requires no stock-picking skill and is mechanically trivial, yet is rarely run by small accounts who default to buy-and-hold or single-name bets. Professional CTAs run this with leverage and dozens of futures contracts unavailable to a long-only, no-leverage retail account -- this tests the stripped-down, house-constrained version.
cross_asset_trend.who_loses: Non-forecasting flow: fuel hedgers locking costs regardless of price view, pensions shifting to bonds on a fixed schedule regardless of rate outlook, central banks defending currency bands, sovereign wealth funds rebalancing commodity allocations on policy schedules rather than return forecasts.

bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: CostModel commission 0.0bps + slippage 5.0bps per side, min ticket $25.0; realized annual turnover 20.00x, cost drag 200bps/yr. Survivor reruns at 2x slippage required before any verdict.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Structural: snapshots read same-day DAILY rows only; select() sees bars through the Friday signal close with signal_lag=1 (fills at next trading day's close); quarterly membership applies only from the first trading day after its quarter-end; MA exits evaluate on the day's own raw close and fill at that close. No event feeds used.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: n_trials=3, all enumerated in the committed prereg (primary + no-exit + monthly ablations); nothing else was run against this panel in this study. House counter hypotheses_ever after this record: 96 (93 prior; +1 slug registration +2 manual for the ablation cells, gauntlet_v1 counting precedent). The verdict criterion is benchmark-relative sign, not a Sharpe race, so the n=3 selection effect cannot manufacture a pass.
bias_checklist.overfitting: Parameters per cell fixed by prereg; No in-study tuning; parameters are Delphi's live frozen values; 3 cells enumerated in the prereg. Sharpe 0.15 with bootstrap 95% CI [-0.21, 0.52]; parameter_cliff_report on the grid distinguishes plateaus from isolated peaks before any cell is believed.
bias_checklist.regime: Window spans dot-com bust, 2003-07 bull, GFC, 2013-19 bull, covid, 2021-26 momentum era. Per-regime table published: the ruleset lost money or lagged in EVERY regime before 2013 (1999-2002: -16.5%/yr; 2003-07: -0.7%; 2008-12: -5.9%) and only shone 2020-2021 (+29.4%/yr) and 2021-26 (+12.2%/yr) — the #4 window is the OUTLIER, exactly the era-dependence this study was designed to expose.
bias_checklist.selection: No in-study tuning; parameters are Delphi's live frozen values; 3 cells enumerated in the prereg. The population/universe rule and every cell were enumerated in the prereg before any panel data was pulled; nothing was added after results were seen.
bias_checklist.small_n: n_obs=6913 daily returns; Sharpe 0.15 (bootstrap 95% CI [-0.21, 0.52]); total return 34.3%. Forward validation still requires >=20 graded paper trades on the shrunk mean regardless of in-sample n.
bias_checklist.survivorship: Panel: Sharadar SEP for the union of 110 quarterly PIT top-119 lists (419 final tickers, 2.06M bars, 1998-12..2026-06), delisted members through their final trading day; universes from DAILY quarter-end cross-sections incl. names that later died. 0 union members missing bars; 0 price-return-only symbols (full closeadj coverage). 25 distinct ADR/Canadian mega-caps sat above the top-119 floor and were excluded BY DESIGN (US-common-only rule inherited from #4), verified name-by-name against TICKERS categories — a US-common universe definition, not a coverage hole.

backtest.notes: Primary cell (live ruleset): 1.08% CAGR, Sharpe 0.15 [CI -0.21, 0.52], maxDD -76.7%, excess vs EW-119 -6.86%/yr (IR -0.43), vs SPY -7.60%/yr. No-exit ablation: 6.92% CAGR — the 20-day-MA daily exit COST ~5.8pp/yr (4,552 MA-exit round trips, 32% win, -0.52% mean vs rebalance exits 81% win, +7.9% mean): the runbook's 'the exit made the backtest work' claim is inverted at full window. Monthly ablation: -0.61%. Benchmark EW-119: 7.94%, maxDD -55.1% (the exit did not even shrink book drawdown). 2x slippage: -0.93% CAGR. Turnover 20x/yr, cost drag ~200bps/yr. mean_excess = mean of 3 cells' excess-vs-EW (-6.86, -1.01, -8.54)/3. Per-cell artifacts docs/data/delphi_fullwindow/.

delphi_ruleset_fullwindow.falsifiable_claim: SUPPORTED iff the primary cell's full-window net excess CAGR > 0 vs BOTH the EW top-119 benchmark and SPY total return at 1x costs; REFUTED iff excess vs EW <= 0.
delphi_ruleset_fullwindow.mechanism: Confirmatory measurement of Delphi's live frozen ruleset (65-day momentum, top-10 equal-weight, 20-day-MA daily exit, weekly rebalance, 5-trading-day cooldown, 5% cash floor) over the full 1999-2026 Sharadar panel on blind quarterly PIT top-119 universes. The claimed edge: cross-sectional momentum among mega-caps, with the MA trailing exit transforming momentum's crash profile (the runbook credits the exit for the backtest). gauntlet_v1 killed exit-less momentum 2000-2015; #4 measured her rules strong 2021-2026 — this study decides rules vs era.

prereg.metric: Net-of-cost full-window excess CAGR vs same-universe EW benchmark and SPY TR; IR; per-regime splits; per-exit-reason round trips.
prereg.population_definition: Quarter-end top-119 US common stocks by Sharadar DAILY marketcap, 1999-03-31..2026-06-30 (110 quarters), resolved to final tickers via layered window-aware TICKERS sweep; members' SEP bars 1998-12 onward, delisted included through final bar; ADR/Canadian issuers excluded by design.
prereg.success_criteria: PASS: primary cell excess CAGR > 0 vs both benchmarks net at 1x slippage. REFUTED: excess vs EW benchmark <= 0. 3 cells enumerated (primary, no-exit, monthly); 2x-slippage rerun disclosed, not gating.

delphi_ruleset_fullwindow.underutilized_because: Not underutilized — mega-cap momentum is crowded; the open question was whether the exit overlay and weekly cadence rescue an otherwise-dead family.
delphi_ruleset_fullwindow.who_loses: Late reactors anchoring on stale prices, per the standard momentum story; and if the exit works, crash-riders who hold through momentum unwinds.

bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: Per-bucket linear slippage (LARGE 5bps, SMALL 25bps), $25 min ticket, $3 price floor, $1M median-21d-dollar-volume floor, at $10k/cell -- house scale, where these names are tradable. Prereg-mandated 2x-slippage disclosure on the five holdout cells: vol_L126__N10__LARGE: IS 9.15%/HO 8.52% at 2x slippage; vol_L126__N25__LARGE: IS 9.27%/HO 6.69% at 2x slippage; vol_L126__N50__LARGE: IS 9.48%/HO 8.55% at 2x slippage; vol_L126__N50__SMALL: IS 8.85%/HO 4.13% at 2x slippage; vol_L63__N25__LARGE: IS 8.81%/HO 8.76% at 2x slippage. Verdicts unchanged at 2x.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Signals computed from raw closeadj through the month-end signal close t; execution at the NEXT month's first trading close via explicit signal/execution date pairs (engine signal_lag feature, added before any run; carry-forward fills price executions but can never satisfy signal-eligibility windows). Marketcap dated t derives from t's close and share count -- used for t's signal, traded t+1. No fundamentals, no restatement exposure.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: 90 trials by design, all counted: hypotheses_ever went 1 -> 91 at prereg time (house counter, registry adjustment record). DSR benchmarked against n_trials=90 with cross-sectional variance_of_sr=0.0556 measured on the grid's own 90 in-sample Sharpes. The holdout was touched exactly once, by the top-5 only. No cell beyond the 90 was ever computed.
bias_checklist.overfitting: Zero free parameters after the prereg commit: windows, N, buckets, floors, costs, splits and bars all frozen before data. 186 monthly rebalances and ~3,900 daily observations per cell in-sample; the <=5-survivor cap bounded selection intensity on the 120-month holdout. The refutation itself is the overfitting demonstration: 10 cells cleared a deflated in-sample bar and all 5 advancing cells failed out-of-sample against the benchmark.
bias_checklist.regime: In-sample 2000-2015 contains the dot-com crash and GFC; holdout 2016-2025 is one long bull with a fast COVID crash. Prereg section 9.7 named clearing both as the regime test; low-vol passed the crash-heavy sample and failed the bull decade against the benchmark -- the refutation IS the regime finding: the family's in-sample edge was crash exposure, not persistent alpha.
bias_checklist.selection: Population = every name passing the prereg's mechanical screens on each of 186 IS + 120 HO month-ends (no hand-picking; catalog saved as house population gauntlet_v1_universes, 612 rows with coverage note). The grid itself was the complete pre-committed catalog of variants: 90 cells enumerated in the prereg before any panel pull, none added or removed after.
bias_checklist.small_n: Per cell: ~3,900 IS daily returns, 186 IS + 120 HO monthly rebalances; DSR/PSR computed with each cell's own n_obs, skew and kurtosis. Factory-level mean_excess is the mean IS CAGR gap vs bucket benchmark across all 90 cells (-5.5%/yr, 14/90 positive); shrinkage on n=90 leaves the sign untouched. No forward test follows -- only a supported backtest earns one.
bias_checklist.survivorship: Sharadar SEP/DAILY panels include delisted names through their final trading day (verified SIVBQ/BBBYQ); universes built per-date from names alive that day. 27.2M IS bars across 14,240 tickers, 0 skipped. DAILY-vs-SEP mismatch: median 3 names/date (0.06%, max 16) excluded and disclosed. Delisting exits sellable at stale final close at next monthly rebalance -- optimistic, which can only have UNDERSTATED the refuted families' failure.

backtest.notes: Factory-level record: n=90 grid cells; mean_excess = mean in-sample CAGR excess vs same-bucket EW benchmark across the grid. Stage 1 killed 80/90 (all momentum/reversal/size/neglect); stage 2 (one holdout pass, top-5 low-vol) killed the rest: PSR vs zero = 1.0 for all five, but none beat the bucket benchmark 2016-2025. Per-cell data: docs/data/gauntlet_v1/.

gauntlet_v1.falsifiable_claim: At least one of the 90 pre-committed cells clears the frozen in-sample bar (DSR >= 0.95 with n_trials=90 AND net CAGR above the matching equal-weight bucket benchmark) and then the frozen holdout bar (PSR >= 0.95 AND beats the benchmark out-of-sample, net of costs). Zero passers at either stage refutes the factory.
gauntlet_v1.mechanism: Factory-level meta-hypothesis: the best-documented cross-sectional equity anomalies — momentum (investor underreaction and slow information diffusion), short-term reversal (compensation for providing liquidity into price pressure), low-volatility (leverage-constrained investors overpaying for lottery-like high-vol names), and size/neglect (institutional mandates and analyst coverage thinning out below the top ~1000 names, leaving mispricings unharvested) — are each claimed by decades of academic literature to persist in US equities. Each grid cell is the simplest implementable long-only form of one such claim. The factory tests whether ANY survive realistic costs, a 16y in-sample, a 10y holdout, and deflation for 90 simultaneous tries at the house's own scale.

prereg.metric: Net-of-cost daily equity curve per cell (signals at month-end close t, execution at close of first trading day of the next month, per-bucket slippage); annualized Sharpe -> deflated Sharpe (n_trials=90) in-sample, PSR on holdout; net CAGR vs matching equal-weight bucket benchmark.
prereg.population_definition: Monthly point-in-time universes from the full Sharadar panel (SEP bars + DAILY marketcap, delisted names included through final trading day), 1998-12-01 to 2025-12-31: at each month-end signal date, names with both a DAILY marketcap and SEP bar, unadjusted close >= $3, median 21d dollar volume >= $1M; LARGE = marketcap ranks 1-500, SMALL = ranks 501-2000. Ranks 2001+ out of scope by design. Full construction rules frozen in the prereg doc, section 3.
prereg.success_criteria: Stage 1 (all 90 cells, in-sample executions 2000-07..2015-12): survive iff DSR >= 0.95 AND net CAGR > bucket benchmark; top 5 survivors by DSR advance. Stage 2 (holdout executions 2016-01..2025-12, touched once): pass iff PSR vs 0 >= 0.95 AND net CAGR > benchmark holdout CAGR. Factory supported iff >=1 stage-2 passer; refuted iff zero at either stage; inconclusive only for >20% coverage failure. Survivors then face the standard >=20-grade paper forward gate on shrunk mean.

gauntlet_v1.underutilized_because: Not underutilized in the aggregate — these are famous factors — but famously fragile after costs, in long-only implementable form, at small size, outside backtest samples. The factory's point is precisely to measure how much of the published effect is harvestable by THIS house, and to build the graveyard of cells that are not.
gauntlet_v1.who_loses: Momentum: late reactors who anchor on stale prices. Reversal: impatient liquidity demanders forced to sell into weakness. Low-vol: leverage-constrained lottery buyers overpaying for volatile names. Size/neglect: nobody is on the other side per se — the edge, if real, is a premium for holding what mandates and coverage ignore.

bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: Not formally modeled (no spread/slippage deduction), but this does not affect the verdict since the raw effect is already negative -- costs would only push it further negative. Many names show extreme reverse-split-adjusted price levels (e.g. VCIG entry/exit in the millions post-adjustment), a tell that this population skews toward chronically distressed, thinly-traded microcaps even before accounting for the 15 that vanished from the data entirely.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Entry fixed at trading-day 185 from each name's own first trade -- a count-based rule using only information knowable at IPO time (the 180-day lockup term is boilerplate stated in the S-1/424B4 before day 0). No name was included or excluded based on anything that happened after its own IPO date; SPY's return uses the identical calendar dates as each stock's own entry/exit, not a shared window.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: This is hypothesis #1 in lab['hypotheses_ever'] -- the first strategy ever registered in this lab. No prior variant of this idea was tried against this or any other dataset before this cut.
bias_checklist.overfitting: Two knobs total (185-trading-day entry offset, 60-trading-day hold), both fixed by the mechanism (lockup length + a round-number hold) in the prereg before any data was seen. Neither knob was adjusted after seeing the result -- this is the first and only cut on this design; a redesign requires a new slug and fresh prereg.
bias_checklist.regime: Single sample: 2023 IPO class held into 2024, a disinflation/soft-landing rally regime for SPY (each name's own SPY window returned +2% to +19%). Even against a rising market the median small-cap IPO fell -33% over the post-lockup 60-day window -- this looks like the well-documented long-run small-cap-IPO underperformance continuing past the lockup date, not a distinct reversal at it. Untested in a down-market regime, but the negative sign does not obviously depend on this being a bull-market sample.
bias_checklist.selection: Population built from the complete public 2023 IPO calendar (154 rows, stockanalysis.com), filtered by mechanical rules (SPAC-name pattern, spinoff, foreign cross-listing, missing price, price>$10) applied uniformly before any return was computed. IPO price<=$10 was used as an operational proxy for the prereg's '<$300M offer size' criterion rather than per-name S-1 totals -- verifying exact offer size for ~150 candidates individually would have cost as many tool calls as the rest of the study, for a threshold that already cleanly separates the list (obscure microcaps vs. the year's recognized large IPOs, no ambiguous middle case observed).
bias_checklist.small_n: Raw n=65, above the prereg's n>=30 floor. Shrunk mean (prior_n=20, prior_mean=0) is -8.01%, close to the raw -10.41% since n=65 dominates the prior -- shrinkage does not rescue this toward zero in any meaningful way. No forward test follows, since only a 'supported' backtest earns one under proteus/lab.py.
bias_checklist.survivorship: 15/80 (18.75%) of the population could not be priced because the broker's historicals API does not serve bars for tickers now inactive/renamed/delisted, regardless of whether real trading data existed during the 2024 entry/exit window. Of 5 spot-checked via WebSearch, all 5 were distress outcomes (Nasdaq delisting-for-cause, forced reverse splits, business-combination exits, sub-$1 collapse) -- meaning the 65-name result almost certainly OVERSTATES the true population mean excess, not understates it. A free alternate data source (stooq.com) was unreachable (503 via proxy); stockanalysis.com's history page redirects a renamed ticker to its successor's current price series rather than the pre-rename history.

backtest.notes: Median excess -33.07%, win rate 18/65=27.7%, t=-0.62. Result is dominated in sign only by a single +1011.53% biotech outlier (BMR) that exceeds the entire net excess in magnitude -- excluding it, the other 64 names average roughly -25.9% excess. Fails the supported-criterion 'no single event >40% of total excess' on its own even before considering the negative mean.

ipo_lockup_reversion.falsifiable_claim: A mechanical, non-discretionary long entry exactly 185 trading days after IPO pricing (5 sessions after the standard 180-day lockup expires) across a complete population of small-cap (sub-$300M offer size) US IPOs, held 60 trading days, produces mean excess return vs SPY that is reliably positive (t>=1.5, n>=30); if mean excess is at or below zero, or the raw n is unreachable/inconclusive, the hypothesis is refuted or inconclusive as tested.
ipo_lockup_reversion.mechanism: Standard IPO underwriting agreements impose a ~180-day lockup restricting pre-IPO holders (founders, employees, VC/PE funds) from selling. This is a fixed, calendar-certain, information-free event: on expiry, tradeable float mechanically increases as previously-restricted shares become sellable, independent of any change in the issuer's fundamentals. Anticipatory selling and short-side positioning into the well-known 'lockup cliff' narrative depresses price into and just after expiry as a pure supply/liquidity shock, not a valuation reassessment. Because the shock carries no new information, once the initial wave of lockup-driven selling is absorbed by the market (typically within 1-2 weeks), price should partially revert toward its pre-shock trend as the temporary oversupply clears and the stock re-prices on fundamentals rather than the float shock.

prereg.metric: Simple holding-period return minus SPY's simple return over the same calendar dates ('excess return'), entry T+185 trading days, 60-trading-day hold.
prereg.population_definition: All US primary equity IPOs (common stock, standard underwritten IPO; excludes SPACs, closed-end funds, ADRs, uplistings, direct listings) that priced in calendar year 2023, with total offer size < $300M, sourced from a complete public IPO calendar for that year (not a hand-picked subset). Delisted/failed names are included, not dropped.
prereg.success_criteria: Supported: n>=30, mean excess > 0, t>=1.5, no single event >40% of total excess. Refuted: mean excess<=0, or t<1.0. Else inconclusive.

ipo_lockup_reversion.underutilized_because: Lockup-expiry research and financial media coverage concentrate almost entirely on famous, mega-cap IPOs (exactly the kind session 3 rejected on EQPT/SPCX as already-published and priced-in); the broad population of small-cap, low-coverage IPOs has calendar-certain, EDGAR-documented lockup dates that are public but that no systematic screener or analyst desk tracks, leaving the effect unarbitraged specifically in names too small and unglamorous to attract the attention that kills it in famous names.
ipo_lockup_reversion.who_loses: Momentum shorts and lockup-cliff narrative traders who pile onto the anticipated overhang and are then squeezed as selling exhausts and price reverts; also pre-IPO holders forced to sell into a temporarily depressed, oversupplied tape rather than at a fundamentals-clearing price.

quiet_cluster_ghost.falsifiable_claim: Quiet-cluster mean 12-month excess (vs IWM) > 0 with t>=2 on n>=30 graded events validates; mean excess <=0 or t<2 with n>=30 refutes; n<30 stays inconclusive. Fresh Form 4 population from 2026-06-01 onward only (spent replay data excluded by construction).
quiet_cluster_ghost.mechanism: Backlog #1: insider clusters that fire with no contemporaneous 8-K, no abnormal price reaction, and no volume anomaly at the knowability date may carry more private conviction than clusters the market already reacted to at filing -- the raw (unfiltered) cluster population measured -6.4%/yr at 12mo in the spent 2025-01-01..2026-05-31 replay; this tests whether the quiet subset differs.

prereg.metric: 12-month (and interim 6-month) excess return vs IWM per quiet-cluster event, entry at first close on/after knowability date+5 calendar days.
prereg.population_definition: Every Form 4 open-market-buy cluster (>=2 distinct insiders, each >=$10k, aggregate >=$50k, 60-calendar-day window, grant-mill >60-filing issuers excluded) filed EDGAR-wide from 2026-06-01 onward, filtered to the subset with no 8-K within +/-3 trading days of knowability, |return|<2% on knowability day and next day, and volume <1.5x trailing-21d median on knowability day.
prereg.success_criteria: Validated: mean 12mo excess>0, t>=2, n>=30. Refuted: mean excess<=0 or t<2 at n>=30. Inconclusive: n<30 -- still accruing, no early verdict.

quiet_cluster_ghost.underutilized_because: The full cluster signal is public (every Form 4 is EDGAR-visible) but almost nobody filters clusters by the ABSENCE of contemporaneous news/price/volume signal, since that requires a three-way negative screen most scanners are not built to run at all; if the quiet subset differs, it is underused because it is operationally annoying to isolate, not because it is secret.
quiet_cluster_ghost.who_loses: If real: market participants who only act on visible catalysts (news, volume, price pops) and structurally miss the quiet subset until the re-rating catches up -- the same late-reactor story as momentum, but at the filing-conviction layer rather than the price layer.

delphi_ruleset_faithful.mechanism: CORRECTION study for delphi_ruleset_fullwindow, whose primary cell mis-specified Delphi's rules (erratum in its results doc). This slug tests her ACTUAL semantics, mirrored line-for-line from delphi/signals.py::rank_by_momentum and delphi/backtest.py: the 20-day MA as an ENTRY FILTER inside the momentum ranking (below-MA names never ranked), 65-day momentum and MA on the same split-adjusted close series, top-10 equal weight, 20% rebalance band, cooldown-on-any-sell, daily (current live cadence) and weekly (original claim) evaluation, on the same 110 PIT top-119 universes.
delphi_ruleset_faithful.who_loses: Late reactors anchoring on stale prices, per the standard momentum story — if the edge exists at all in implementable long-only form.
delphi_ruleset_faithful.underutilized_because: Not underutilized; the open question was whether her true entry-filter design escapes the momentum graveyard where #11's strawman variant could not.
delphi_ruleset_faithful.falsifiable_claim: SUPPORTED iff faithful_daily full-window net excess CAGR > 0 vs BOTH the EW top-119 benchmark and SPY TR at 1x costs; REFUTED iff excess vs the EW benchmark <= 0.

prereg.population_definition: Identical panel to delphi_ruleset_fullwindow: 110 quarter-end PIT top-119 US-common universes 1999-03-31..2026-06-30, 419 final tickers, SEP bars 1998-12 onward with delisted members through final bar; ADR/Canadian excluded by design; panel reuse disclosed as the house's erratum-correction path.
prereg.metric: Net-of-cost full-window excess CAGR vs same-universe EW benchmark and SPY TR; IR; per-regime splits; per-reason round trips; 2x rerun.
prereg.success_criteria: PASS: faithful_daily excess CAGR > 0 vs both benchmarks net at 1x slippage. REFUTED: excess vs EW <= 0. Two cells enumerated (daily primary, weekly); no other variants.

bias_checklist.survivorship: Same panel as delphi_ruleset_fullwindow: 419 final tickers, 2.06M SEP bars, delisted members through final trading day, 0 missing, 0 price-return-only; universes from DAILY quarter-end cross-sections with the self-auditing top-119 boundary (only above-floor exclusions: 25 ADR/Canadian issuers excluded by the US-common-only design rule, verified per name).
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Structural: same-day-only snapshots, bars trimmed to the signal date, signal_lag=1 (signals at close t, fills at close t+1 — deliberately STRICTER than her live code's same-day execution, which is a small lookahead this study refuses); quarterly membership applies from the first trading day after its quarter-end; MA filter computed on the signal date's own closes.
bias_checklist.selection: Two cells enumerated in the committed correction prereg BEFORE any faithful cell was computed (the runner phase did not exist at prereg commit). Parameters are Delphi's pre-existing live values throughout; the correction was specified from her code, not from candidate results.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: n_trials=2 this study; cumulative Delphi-family cells on this panel now 5 across two preregs, all enumerated. House counter hypotheses_ever after this record: 98. Verdict criterion is benchmark-relative sign — a 5-cell selection effect cannot manufacture a pass, and every one of the 5 failed anyway.
bias_checklist.overfitting: Zero parameters tuned in-study. Sharpe CI for the primary [-0.35, 0.39] straddles zero; the verdict rests on a -9.36pp/yr benchmark gap, not a threshold-adjacent statistic.
bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: 5bps/side mega-cap slippage + $25 tickets, band damping modeled (trims gated at ±20%); realized turnover 37.4x/yr on the daily cell (374bps/yr drag), 18.8x weekly; 2x-slippage rerun: -5.04% CAGR, verdict unchanged. The design's own churn, not the cost assumption, is the story.
bias_checklist.regime: 27-year window spanning six prereg-frozen regimes. Published table: 1999-2002 -25.7%/yr, 2003-07 -1.3%, 2008-12 -11.2%, 2013-19 +6.4%, 2020-21 +27.2%, 2021-26 +13.6%/yr (daily cell). The weekly cell's 2021-26 segment (+18.89%/yr) replicates #4's Run B (+19.15%/yr implied) within 0.3pp — cross-study validation that the era, not the rules, made that window.
bias_checklist.small_n: n_obs=6,913 daily returns; 10,971 round trips (median hold 3 trading days) on the primary cell; sharpe_ci published. No forward test follows — only supported backtests earn one, and this is refuted terminal.

backtest.notes: faithful_daily (primary, current live cadence): -1.42% CAGR, Sharpe 0.01 [CI -0.35, 0.39], maxDD -90.5%, excess vs EW-119 -9.36%/yr (IR -0.58), vs SPY -10.10%/yr; -5.04% CAGR at 2x slippage. faithful_weekly (original claim's cadence): 0.74% CAGR, excess -7.20%/yr, maxDD -91.9%. Benchmark EW-119: 7.94%, maxDD -55.1%. Her true MA-entry-filter design churns HARDER than #11's mis-specified variant (names oscillate around their MA in and out of the eligible set; band cannot damp full dropouts): 10,971 round trips, median hold 3 trading days, 37.4x/yr turnover. mean_excess = mean of 2 cells (-9.36, -7.20)/2. Reconciliation: faithful_weekly 2021-26 = +18.89%/yr vs #4 Run B implied +19.15%/yr — the two studies agree; the era was real, and it was the outlier. Artifacts docs/data/delphi_fullwindow/faithful_results.json.

cef_tender_convergence.mechanism: When a closed-end fund files an issuer tender offer (SC TO-I), the fund itself becomes a CONSTRAINED buyer: bound by its own filed terms to repurchase shares at an NAV-linked price (typically 98-100% of NAV) in an offer that must stay open at least 20 business days (rule 14e-1(a)). If the fund trades at a discount, the tender creates a quasi-contractual convergence path from market price toward the NAV-linked terminal value. Gates: G1 pass (filing-driven, not tape), G2 pass (the counterparty is the fund, the constraint is its own SC TO-I), G3 pass (CEFs are thin, tenders often odd-lot-prioritized, institutions cannot size in), G4 pass (contractual terminal value), G5 expected-pass (dense recurring event stream).
cef_tender_convergence.who_loses: Nobody is outsmarted: the fund is a price-insensitive buyer BY ITS OWN FILED TERMS; sellers pre-expiry are liquidity-demanding holders exiting before the NAV-linked price is available.
cef_tender_convergence.underutilized_because: Position sizes are capped by tiny fund ADVs and odd-lot mechanics — the research cost per deployable dollar exceeds any institution's threshold; it does not exceed ours.
cef_tender_convergence.falsifiable_claim: SUPPORTED iff shrunk mean market-adjusted CAR at +25 trading days > +1.0% with t>=2 and n>=30 across the complete 2020-2026 SC TO-I catalog; REFUTED iff shrunk mean <= 0 at n>=30.

prereg.population_definition: ALL SC TO-I filings (excluding /A amendments) 2020-01-01..2026-05-15 via EDGAR full-text search, filer matched to listed CEFs (Sharadar SFP category CEF), one event per fund per 180 days, unmatched events disclosed as unpriceable — complete catalog, defined before it exists.
prereg.metric: Market-adjusted CAR vs SPY at offsets 0-25 trading days from first close strictly after filing date (shared.gauntlet.event_car); primary statistic shrunk mean CAR(25).
prereg.success_criteria: SUPPORTED: shrunk CAR(25) > +1.0%, t>=2, n>=30 -> earns paper forward test. REFUTED: shrunk CAR(25) <= 0 at n>=30. Else INCONCLUSIVE. Secondary discount>=12% cut preregistered, non-gating.

bias_checklist.survivorship: Population from EDGAR quarterly full-index (form.idx) — filings, not surviving funds; 3,010 raw SC TO-I rows 2020-01..2026-05, 153 CEF-matched events (+44 same-tender dupes deduped at 180d), 2,813 unmatched filers are operating companies and UNLISTED interval funds (no market price — out of scope by the frozen 'listed' definition; top unmatched are quarterly repurchase vehicles). Delisted-after-tender funds keep SFP bars through final day.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Entry at first close STRICTLY AFTER the filing acceptance date; the listing screen (>=15 bars in the 30 calendar days BEFORE filing) uses pre-event data only. Note: funds often press-release tenders before the SC TO-I lands — our entry is thus conservative/late, which cannot manufacture the negative result (it can only have missed an earlier pop).
bias_checklist.selection: Complete catalog from full-index sweeps (no full-text query bias); window, dedup, and match rules frozen in the prereg before the catalog existed. One in-flight correction disclosed: a company-name parsing bug initially broke the name-match fallback; fixing it added 24 events BEFORE any outcome was computed.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: One primary statistic; the preregistered secondary (stated-NAV discount>=12% cut) was NOT run — slug went terminal-refuted on the primary, mooting it. Counter +2 at record per the prereg's own commitment (hypotheses_ever 98->100 including registration). Verdict is a frozen sign+t bar, not a selected best cut.
bias_checklist.overfitting: Zero tuned parameters: offset 25 is the statutory minimum offer window; thresholds frozen pre-data. The listing screen is the frozen population definition ('listed CEF') made operational after sparse-quote vehicles (VCX six identical +314% artifacts, MSIF zero 2024 bars, EIIA weekly prints, FSCO) surfaced — BOTH raw (n=153, shrunk -0.76%, t -1.88) and listed-only (n=132, shrunk -1.57%, t -3.91) reported; the verdict is the same sign under both.
bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: Event study is gross of costs; costs only worsen a negative result. CEF spreads are wide — a forward test was the venue for net grading and none is earned.
bias_checklist.regime: 2020-2026 spans covid, ZIRP, the 2022 rate shock, two bull legs. Per-year mean CAR(25): 2020 +0.1%, 2021 +0.7%, 2022 +2.9%, 2023 -2.9%, 2024 -1.4%, 2025 +0.2%, 2026 -6.5%(n=5) — no year clears the +1.0% bar; the lone positive year (2022, n=13) is the rate-shock discount-blowout cohort.
bias_checklist.small_n: n=132 listed events (153 raw) with 100% pricing coverage after the listing screen; shrunk mean cited; t=-3.91 — the NEGATIVE drift is itself statistically strong, not a null.

backtest.notes: PRIMARY (listed, n=132): mean market-adjusted CAR(25) -1.81%, shrunk -1.57%, t -3.91, win rate 33.3%; medians match means (no outlier dependence). Raw incl. sparse-quote vehicles (n=153): -0.86%, t -1.88, same sign. The curve is negative from offset 3 and worsens through 20-25 (expiry/proration re-widening window). Not merely 'no edge': post-filing tender windows UNDERPERFORM SPY significantly. Likely mechanism: any convergence is priced at the earlier press release (entry here is the SC TO-I filing), the NAV-linked terminal value applies only to prorated tendered shares, and discounts re-widen into expiry. A press-release-entry variant would be a NEW hypothesis (G5 power fine, but it must pass the gates again with a different event anchor). Artifacts: docs/data/cef_tenders/.

cef_tender_toc_anchor.mechanism: #7 refuted the CEF-tender FILING-date entry and localized the failure to the anchor: the convergence toward the fund's NAV-linked, self-documented repurchase terms is plausibly captured between the ANNOUNCEMENT (SC TO-C communication) and the TO-I launch. Same constrained counterparty (the fund, bound by its own filed offer, rule 14e-1(a) 20-business-day minimum), earlier knowability anchor. Gates: G1-G4 pass as in #7; G5 uncertain (TO-C usage among CEFs unknown; n<30 -> inconclusive, honest).
cef_tender_toc_anchor.who_loses: Sellers between announcement and launch who demand liquidity before the NAV-linked price is available; the fund is price-insensitive by its own filed terms.
cef_tender_toc_anchor.underutilized_because: Same capacity inversion as #7 — thin funds, prorated odd-lot mechanics; research cost per deployable dollar excludes institutions.
cef_tender_toc_anchor.falsifiable_claim: SUPPORTED iff shrunk mean CAR(25) from the TO-C anchor > +1.0%, t>=2, n>=30 quiet events; REFUTED iff shrunk <= 0 at n>=30; INCONCLUSIVE below n=30.

prereg.population_definition: The frozen #7 catalog's 153 CEF events joined to ALL SC TO-C filings by the same CIKs from the same 2020Q1-2026Q2 full-index sweeps; kept iff earliest TO-C >= 1 day before the TO-I; same pre-anchor listing screen.
prereg.metric: Market-adjusted CAR vs SPY at +25 trading days from first close strictly after the earliest TO-C; shared.gauntlet.event_car.
prereg.success_criteria: SUPPORTED: shrunk CAR(25) > +1.0%, t>=2, n>=30. REFUTED: shrunk <= 0 at n>=30. Else INCONCLUSIVE. Overlap with #7's later-anchor outcomes disclosed.

bias_checklist.survivorship: 82/153 frozen CEF events had a prior TO-C within 90 days (coverage disclosed — TO-C usage is self-selected by funds and this subset cannot speak for non-TO-C tenders); same listing screen as #7; delisted funds keep SFP bars.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Entry strictly after the anchor filing's acceptance date; listing screen uses pre-anchor bars only; anchors from EDGAR full-index dates.
bias_checklist.selection: Populations frozen in the committed prereg before the TO-C sweep and before any operating outcome; the #7 catalog reused as frozen infrastructure, outcomes at THIS anchor never previously computed.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: One primary statistic, no secondary run; hypotheses_ever 102->103 with sibling slug's secondary. Frozen bars missed NARROWLY (shrunk +0.81% vs +1.0%; t 1.92 vs 2.0) — recorded INCONCLUSIVE, not talked up to supported.
bias_checklist.overfitting: Zero tuned parameters; offset 25 = statutory window; thresholds frozen pre-data; outlier audit run before recording (medians vs means, top-k exclusion sensitivity).
bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: Gross event study; the +1.0%-shrunk SUPPORTED bar exists partly as a cost buffer; CEF/small-cap spreads named as the reason a forward test grades net.
bias_checklist.regime: Events span 2020-2026; positive lean is broad-based (median +1.52% > mean, win 61%), not one era's outlier — but per-year cells are thin at n=82.
bias_checklist.small_n: n=82; shrunk mean cited; mean excl. top-1 +0.85%, excl. top-3 +0.63% — no single-event dependence. Below the frozen n/effect bars regardless.

backtest.notes: FIRST NON-NEGATIVE READING IN THE HOUSE: mean CAR(25) from the TO-C (announcement) anchor +1.00%, shrunk +0.81%, t 1.92, win 61%, median +1.52% — the sign flip vs #7's TO-I anchor (-1.81%, t -3.91) confirms #7's mechanism reading: the convergence is captured between announcement and launch. INCONCLUSIVE per frozen bars, missed narrowly on both. The honest continuation is FRESH data, not a re-cut: new TO-C events accrue forward from 2026-06; a forward-accrual continuation under the same frozen rule would be a new prereg the operator may commission.

issuer_tender_operating.mechanism: Operating-company issuer self-tenders (SC TO-I, non-CEF): the company itself is the constrained buyer, bound by its own filed dutch-auction range or fixed price for a >=20-business-day window — a quasi-contractual demand floor plus a management signal, documented in the filing. Gates: G1 pass (filing anchor), G2 pass (issuer bound by its own TO-I), G3 partial (mixed caps; preregistered $2B split is the capacity-inversion cut), G4 pass (filed price/range), G5 pass (hundreds of events).
issuer_tender_operating.who_loses: Holders selling into the offer window below the filed range — liquidity demanders facing a disclosed, price-insensitive corporate bid.
issuer_tender_operating.underutilized_because: The small-cap half (the preregistered cut) is where fills are too small for institutions; the large half is the control that discipline demands we report rather than cherry-pick.
issuer_tender_operating.falsifiable_claim: SUPPORTED iff shrunk mean CAR(25) > +1.0%, t>=2, n>=30 on the screened population; REFUTED iff shrunk <= 0 at n>=30; secondary $2B split non-gating.

prereg.population_definition: The same 3,010-row SC TO-I catalog minus CEF events; CIK->ticker via SEC company_tickers then exact-name match into full Sharadar SEP TICKERS (delisted included, window-checked) so dead filers are recovered; US common classes only; 180-day per-CIK dedup; unmatched disclosed.
prereg.metric: Market-adjusted CAR vs SPY at +25 trading days from first close strictly after the TO-I filing date; SEP bars (closeadj); shared.gauntlet.event_car.
prereg.success_criteria: SUPPORTED: shrunk CAR(25) > +1.0%, t>=2, n>=30 (screened). REFUTED: shrunk <= 0 at n>=30. Secondary: market-cap split at $2B, non-gating.

bias_checklist.survivorship: 227 matched (206 after listing screen) of 2,797 non-CEF filers; the unmatched bulk is non-traded vehicles (BDC/interval/LLC funds 2,106, non-traded REITs), by-design foreign-issuer exclusions (DSX-class), plus rename misses (Assertio-class) — liquid listed filers (MCK, MGM, DISH, SLM) matched. Under-sampling of later-renamed/acquired filers disclosed as the residual survivorship caveat.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Entry strictly after the anchor filing's acceptance date; listing screen uses pre-anchor bars only; anchors from EDGAR full-index dates.
bias_checklist.selection: Populations frozen in the committed prereg before the TO-C sweep and before any operating outcome; the #7 catalog reused as frozen infrastructure, outcomes at THIS anchor never previously computed.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: One primary + the preregistered $2B capacity split (run): counter includes it; house counter now 103.
bias_checklist.overfitting: Zero tuned parameters; offset 25 = statutory window; thresholds frozen pre-data; outlier audit run before recording (medians vs means, top-k exclusion sensitivity).
bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: Gross event study; the +1.0%-shrunk SUPPORTED bar exists partly as a cost buffer; CEF/small-cap spreads named as the reason a forward test grades net.
bias_checklist.regime: 2020-2026; negative in aggregate and in BOTH capacity halves — small -2.33%/t -1.08, large -0.78%/t -0.56; the G3 rescue the prereg named did not appear.
bias_checklist.small_n: n=206 screened; shrunk -1.66%; sign-based REFUTED bar met at 6.9x the n floor.

backtest.notes: Mean CAR(25) -1.82%, shrunk -1.66%, t -1.20, win 44%; small-cap half WORSE (-2.33%) — the capacity-inversion hope refuted alongside the headline. Operating self-tenders post-filing look like the CEF TO-I result: announcement value, if any, is spent by the filing; the offer window itself carries no harvestable drift at this anchor.

cef_toc_anchor_2014_19.mechanism: Out-of-sample replication of cef_tender_toc_anchor's announcement-anchor rule on the untouched 2014-2019 window: same constrained counterparty (the fund, bound by its own filed tender terms), same earliest-TO-C anchor, same 25-day statutory window, zero re-tuned parameters. Window 1 (2020-26) read +1.00%/t 1.92/win 61% with a rise-then-full-reversal hump peaking near day 25; this window tests both the level and the hump shape in a different rate/activist regime. Gates inherited from the parent slug (G1-G4 pass; G5 solved by replication instead of slow forward accrual).
cef_toc_anchor_2014_19.who_loses: Liquidity-demanding sellers between announcement and launch, facing a price-insensitive NAV-linked corporate bid documented in the TO-C/TO-I.
cef_toc_anchor_2014_19.underutilized_because: Same capacity inversion: thin funds, prorated tenders, research cost per deployable dollar below institutional thresholds only for us.
cef_toc_anchor_2014_19.falsifiable_claim: SUPPORTED iff shrunk mean CAR(25) > +1.0%, t>=2, n>=30 on screened 2014-2019 events; REFUTED iff shrunk <= 0 at n>=30; else INCONCLUSIVE. Hump-shape expectation (reversal by +40) stated in advance as a secondary check.

prereg.population_definition: SC TO-I by listed CEFs 2014-01-01..2019-12-31 from EDGAR quarterly full-index (exact form type, /A excluded), 180d per-fund dedup, SFP category=CEF matching with delisted included and window-checked name fallback; kept iff an SC TO-C by the same CIK exists 1-90d before; pre-anchor listing screen; unpriceable disclosed.
prereg.metric: Market-adjusted CAR vs SPY TR at +25 trading days from first close strictly after the earliest TO-C; full curve to +40 published for the hump-shape check.
prereg.success_criteria: SUPPORTED: shrunk CAR(25) > +1.0%, t>=2, n>=30 -> earns start_forward_test. REFUTED: shrunk <= 0 at n>=30. Else INCONCLUSIVE.

bias_checklist.survivorship: 120 CEF TO-I events 2014-2019 from full-index; 67 with prior TO-C; ZERO no-bars events (SFP delisted coverage held), 2 screened for sparse pre-anchor quotes — coverage is clean; nothing material sits in the unpriceable bucket.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Identical structure to window 1: entry strictly after the earliest TO-C acceptance date; pre-anchor-only listing screen.
bias_checklist.selection: Window and rule frozen in the committed prereg before any 2014-2019 sweep; zero parameters differ from the parent slug — a pure out-of-sample replication.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: One primary statistic, no secondaries; counter at 104. The parent window's near-miss (+0.81% shrunk, t 1.92) is precisely the kind of reading multiple-window replication exists to check — and it failed the check.
bias_checklist.overfitting: The hump-shape expectation (rise to ~25, reverse by 40) was stated in the prereg IN ADVANCE and did not replicate: the 2014-19 curve is monotonically negative from day 0. The window-1 peak-at-25 was the era, not the mechanism.
bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: Gross event study by design (same as the whole tender family); no cost model was applied, and none is needed for the verdict — real CEF spreads of 20-60bps/side would only deepen a result that is already -1.96% gross.
bias_checklist.regime: 2014-2019 spans taper aftermath, 2015-16 credit stress, 2017 calm, 2018 Q4 crash: per-year means -0.7%, -4.0%, -1.5%, +1.1%, -1.3%, -2.4% — five of six negative; the one positive year (2017, n=9) is small and calm. Combined with window 1 (+1.0%), the announcement-anchor effect is REGIME-DEPENDENT at best: plausibly the 2024 activist/discount-management wave, not a persistent convergence mechanism.
bias_checklist.small_n: n=65 at 2.2x the floor; t -5.34; win 21.5%; medians match means (median -2.16% at 25) — no outlier dependence in either direction.

backtest.notes: REPLICATION FAILED, decisively: mean CAR(25) -1.96%, shrunk -1.50%, t -5.34, win 21.5%, monotonic decline (no hump; the prereg's stated hump expectation refuted alongside the level). The parent thread's +1.00% (2020-26) is now best explained as regime — the 2024 activist tender wave — and earns NO forward test. cef_tender_toc_anchor remains recorded inconclusive on its own window; the tender-anchor family is closed absent genuinely new structure. One hour of replication bought what 2.3 years of forward accrual would have bought, before any paper capital moved.
